title: Oh, Peggy Was A Jolly Lass
content:
'Oh, Peggy was a jolly lass,
        Ye heave ho, boys, ye heave ho!
        She never grudged her Jack a glass,
        Ye heave ho, boys, ye heave ho!
        And when he sailed the raging main,
        She faithful was unto her swain,
        Ye heave ho, boys, ye heave ho!'
meaning:
About: Louisa May Alcott was an American novelist. 
She is best known for the novel Little Women, published in 1868. This novel is loosely based on her childhood experiences with her three sisters.
